summary_en: Year-end dividend raised to ¥23/share from ¥17/share projection; annual dividend ¥43/share (prior ¥37/share). Payout ratio 39.4%, above company policy floor of 30%+. Triggered by upward FY6/26 earnings revision (net income to ¥3,570M from ¥3,090M) driven by property-sale contract finalization and continued productivity gains in care operations.
title_en: Notice on Revision of Full-Year Earnings Forecast and Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase) for FY6/26
translation_en_brief:
Charm Care Corporation revised upward its full-year FY6/26 (year ending June 30, 2026) earnings forecast and dividend projections. Consolidated net income attributable to parent shareholders is now estimated at ¥3,570M, up ¥480M from the prior August 2025 forecast of ¥3,090M. Operating profit and ordinary profit also raised by ¥690M and ¥715M respectively. The revision reflects finalization of a real-estate sale contract (whose timing and impact were previously uncertain at Q3 results announcement on May 8) and continued strong productivity improvements in the core nursing-care business.

The dividend is being increased to an annual total of ¥43 per share, from the prior forecast of ¥37 per share. The year-end (period-end) dividend is raised to ¥23 per share from the prior ¥17 per share projection; the interim dividend remains at ¥20 per share (which included a ¥3 per share 20th-anniversary special dividend). The implied payout ratio is 39.4%, comfortably above the company's stated baseline policy of 30% or higher. Management noted that the increase balances shareholder returns with ongoing growth investment and financial soundness.
